title: microlepia protracta
intro: Microlepia protracta is a type of fern plant native to tropical regions, particularly in India and Southeast Asia, and is commonly known as the "Indian fern". This plant has been utilized in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Microlepia protracta include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Microlepia protracta is typically prepared as an herbal tea, decoction, or powder, and is also used in traditional remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
